arts.ttl: From crisis club to ‘doing a Brentford’: Leyton Orient upbeat before City test
arts.sum: Chairman Nigel Travis welcomes the Premier League champions in the FA Cup with grand plans for the future “I remember going to Maine Road in 1964 with my dad and we got walloped six-nothing,” recalls Nigel Travis. “I think it’s the only time I’ve ever seen us play them.” The Leyton Orient chairman’s account of watching his club face Manchester City is rare among fans of the League One team – the teams have not met since a second-tier 2-2 draw in 1966. Fortunes have diverged since but Orient welcome City to east London on Saturday in upbeat mood and at something of a crossroads. After seven and a half years of steady upward progress since Travis’s consortium took over a club in ruins after relegation to the National League, the chairman has been soliciting fresh investment. A new stadium nearby is being considered and talk has been afoot of aping their former lower division London rivals and “doing a Brentford”. The manager, Richie Wellens, has spoken of “not being ‘little Leyton Orient’ any more”. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Manchester City launch fresh legal challenge against Premier League with 'unlawful' claim
arts.sum: Manchester City lost their challenge against changes to the Premier League's Associated Party Transaction rules last November, but they would like arbitration on the matter

arts.ttl: Guardiola expects Man City to struggle at Leyton Orient
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola has insisted Manchester City will not underestimate Leyton Orient in the FA Cup this weekend. City travel to the League One promotion hopefuls on Saturday lunchtime in the fourth round of the competition. The Premier League champions will start as strong favourites and thrashed lower-league opposition in Salford 8-0 in the previous round. arts.ttl: Dias, Ake and Doku in contention for Orient
arts.sum: City travel to London for our fourth round clash at the Gaughan Group Stadium on Saturday, 8 January at 12:30 (UK) and the boss says he has not decided on his starting line-up with the possibility that Dias, Ake and Doku could all come into contention. Pep Guardiola says Ruben Dias, Nathan Ake and Jeremy Doku could be in contention for our FA Cup clash with Leyton Orient.
